generated_by: LITMUS discovery-study reducer (DESIGN §17 steps 3-4, §19). Synthesized across all 30 study/discovery/catalog-*.json against study/corpus/manifest.json. Cataloguers were blind to control_type; the reducer is not. Frequencies are claim-counts across the corpus (one claim may instantiate multiple archetypes; counts are the reducer's tallies of claims whose predicate matches the archetype FORM, not adjudicated gold).
frequency_rank_basis: approximate count of catalogued claims (across all papers) whose checkable FORM matches the archetype

archetypes.name: prose_vs_table_value_disagreement
archetypes.description: A number stated in prose (abstract/body/conclusion) disagrees with, or cannot be bound to, the value in the table/figure it refers to: a transcribed value mismatch, a min/max range that excludes tabled extremes, a stat reported one way in two places, or an abstract figure with no in-text source. Includes prose-vs-figure-caption and abstract-vs-body.

archetypes.name: internal_arithmetic_recompute
archetypes.description: A reported derived number (difference, ratio, percent, fold-change, sum-to-total, subscript sum, mass/mole/yield, percent-of-N, weighted average, kinematic/geometric consistency) does not equal the recomputation from its own stated inputs, or is confirmed to. The bread-and-butter T0 deterministic check; covers stoichiometry sums, percent recompute, percent-change, and table column/row totals.

archetypes.name: prose_vs_figure_value_extraction
archetypes.description: A claim about a value, ordering, peak/argmax, slope, trend, error-bar overlap, or distribution feature that lives ONLY in a figure (bar height, curve maximum, fitted slope annotation, Nyquist/IRF shape, dose-response ordering, parity-plot MAE). Requires reading numbers/relations off a plot and binding to prose. The dominant non-deterministic frontier across the corpus.

archetypes.name: method_appropriateness_judgment
archetypes.description: Whether a stated method/analysis choice is appropriate (paired vs unpaired t-test, t-test on >2 groups where ANOVA is warranted, DFT functional/basis/k-mesh adequacy, NEB force tolerance, MR-normalization convention, post-hoc covariate adjustment, Sabatier/HSAB/BEP modeling choices, housekeeping-gene normalizer, single-timestep DMC). Calibrated reasoning, not deterministic.

archetypes.name: claim_vs_evidence_support_overreach
archetypes.description: An interpretive/causal/scope conclusion is broader than the in-study evidence supports: affirming-the-null as robustness, attributing a bundle effect to one agent, 'bulk-dominated' without a Nyquist deconvolution, 'efficiency' claimed without runtime data, nm->um extrapolation, absence-of-evidence as evidence-of-absence, comparator framing that understates one's own result.

archetypes.name: statcheck_p_vs_teststatistic
archetypes.description: A reported (F or t or chi2, df, p) triple is internally inconsistent: recomputed p from the test statistic and df does not match the stated p (decision error or value error). Classic statcheck. Also covers p-from-(beta,SE) for regression stars and exact-binomial p-from-(x,n,p0).

archetypes.name: external_literature_citation_fidelity
archetypes.description: A specific value or fact attributed to a cited source (record-efficiency, prior effect size, meta-analytic r, comparator OS/ORR, NREL chart record, lattice/elasticity benchmark, solubility, Nobel-year, prior-art priority) that must be checked against the source. T5 retrieval+entailment; higher false-positive risk.

archetypes.name: fixed_knowledge_constant_or_unit
archetypes.description: A physical constant, unit conversion, standard limit, instrument spec, characteristic energy, nomenclature, or definitional value is checked against fixed external knowledge (Faraday constant, Nernst slope, SQ limit, Si bandgap, EIP-1559 12.5%, N2 vibrational quantum, Au workfunction vs electron affinity, [NTf2] vs triflate naming, EDS X-ray line energy, QoR-15 scale range, CTCAE max grade).

archetypes.name: unit_dimensional_consistency
archetypes.description: Stated units/quantities are dimensionally inconsistent or implausible: an axis labelled in V that must be uV, a 'slope approximately unity' that is annotated 2000, E=V/d field conversion, molarity-vs-mol%, power/flow SEI, F.E. dimensional analysis, gas-target factor, block-rate ratio, BPR/discharge-volume geometry.

archetypes.name: reproducibility_recompute_from_SI_or_data
archetypes.description: A concrete number recomputable only from the SI tables, deposited dataset, or code (SI-table row count / join cardinality, KM IRF, regression re-estimate, Cronbach alpha, exclusion-% from raw counts, parity-plot MAE from data, N^-5/4 finite-size extrapolation needing electron counts). Deferred T6 capability; needs the artifact, not just the PDF.

archetypes.name: cross_table_or_cross_section_value_conflict
archetypes.description: The SAME quantity reported in two tables/sections/figures takes two different values, or a value is reused in the wrong slot (NMI 88.38 vs 83.88 for one model+dataset; ANOVA error-df 133 vs 381 in one model; F=1.58 copied into two rows; HCO/COH reference-base confusion; emotional-state IQR body vs table; Tg/lattice value reused across rows).

archetypes.name: equation_or_mechanism_recompute
archetypes.description: A symbolic/algebraic identity, equation reduction, series expansion, balanced reaction, thermodynamic-cycle reconstruction, or limit is checked for correctness (coth small-x expansion, Eq.18->20/21 reductions, CHE linear-shift ladder, mass/charge balance of dissociation, DFT cycle recompute from SI total energies, Riesz-representer/double-robustness form). Largely chemistry/physics/econ-theory; deterministic but needs a symbolic engine.

archetypes.name: value_bounds_plausibility
archetypes.description: A reported value violates a hard bound or is physically impossible: an SD of 0.97 on a [0,1] accuracy mean; a CTCAE Grade-3 alopecia that does not exist; a wallet-day panel obs exceeding wallets x periods; a Clopper-Pearson CI narrower than exact; a within-group df budget summing above N; a percentage pair summing to 99; a 'hard magnet' Hc that is 1-2 orders too low.

archetypes.name: CI_symmetry_and_effectsize_coherence
archetypes.description: A reported confidence interval is not symmetric about its point estimate, or an effect size (Cohen's d, partial eta^2, Cohen's h, post-hoc power) is inconsistent with the F/t/SD/N it should derive from (eta_p^2 vs F mismatch; CI half-width implying a different df than the p; d_av vs d_z ambiguity; power 77% inconsistent with the stated h=0.95).

archetypes.name: sample_flow_and_df_budget_consistency
archetypes.description: Participant/sample bookkeeping must reconcile: recruited - excluded = analyzed; exclusion-reason subcounts sum to the total; per-condition n (=df+1) sum to N; CONSORT figure matches prose; condition Ns sum to group total; abstract 'recruited' vs methods 'analyzed'. Catches off-by-one df typos and figure-vs-text flow conflicts.

archetypes.name: integrity_provenance_signal
archetypes.description: A surfaced (route-to-human, never scored) integrity/transparency signal: retraction-notice document-type detection, declared image/data duplication, COI/funding disclosure, AI-use disclosure, registered-report deviation, data-availability gating ('upon request' vs deposited), blank methodology section, tortured/boilerplate phrasing ('clean version', 'final sharp exercises'), unassigned major spectral peaks, anomalous reported precision.
